1	---
2	name: blueprint-writer
3	description: Update one blueprint chapter to reflect strategy changes, fill missing definitions or theorems, or align prose with the current Lean structure. Plan-agent-dispatched; one writer per chapter. May spawn reference-retriever mid-session when drafting reveals a missing source.
4	write_domain: "blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: true
7	default_enabled: true
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch one writer per chapter that the blueprint-reviewer's
10	    checklist flags as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing
11	    multi-route coverage.
12	  - Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that
13	    matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with
14	    enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and
15	    explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond
16	    what the directive lists.
17	  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
18	    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
19	    writer with TWO globs:
20	      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
21	      --write-domain 'references/**'
22	    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
23	    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
24	    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
25	    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
26	    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
27	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
28	    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
29	    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
30	    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
31	  - After a significant writer round, re-dispatch blueprint-reviewer
32	    in the same iteration to confirm the updated blueprint is now
33	    sufficient — do not assume the writer fixed everything.
34	  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
35	    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
36	    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
37	    strategy-level issue.
38	  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
39	    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the
40	    writer's output — the writer relied on them, so should you.
41	---
42	
43	# Blueprint Writer
44	
45	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
46	
47	## Your Job
48	
49	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
50	
51	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
52	
53	## Directive Format
54	
55	```markdown
56	# Blueprint Writer Directive
57	
58	## Slug
59	<slug>
60	
61	## Target chapter
62	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
63	
64	## Strategy context
65	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
66	
67	## Required content
68	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
69	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
70	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
71	- ...
72	
73	## Out of scope
74	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
75	
76	## References
77	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
78	- ...
79	
80	## Expected outcome
81	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
82	```
83	
84	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
85	
86	## Chapter format
87	
88	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
89	
90	```latex
91	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
92	  \label{thm:some_label}
93	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
94	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
95	  Informal statement of the theorem, using standard mathematical notation.
96	\end{theorem}
97	
98	\begin{proof}
99	  \uses{thm:another_result}
100	  Step-by-step informal proof. Reference blueprint labels with \uses{...}
101	  so the dependency graph stays accurate.
102	\end{proof}
103	```
104	
105	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
106	
107	## Rules
108	
109	### What you CAN do
110	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
111	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
112	- Add `\uses{...}` cross-references.
113	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
114	
115	### What you MUST do
116	- **Keep the chapter valid LaTeX.** Don't leave dangling `\begin{...}` without matching `\end{...}`. Compile-checking is the plan agent's responsibility but you must not introduce syntax errors.
117	- **Stay within your chapter.** Your declared write-domain is one `*.tex` file. The Archon CLI rejects writes outside it.
118	- **Define non-standard macros in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`** before using them — but: that file is outside your write-domain, so you DON'T touch it. If the directive requires a new macro, you note in your report "needs macro `\foo`" and leave the LaTeX using the new command name; the plan agent adds the macro before next iter's typeset.
119	- **Use mathematical, not Lean-syntactic prose.** Describe the proof in the language of mathematics — definitions, set inclusions, ring maps, universal properties — not in Lean tactic syntax. The prover formalizes your math.
120	- **Document every change** in your report.
121	
122	### What you MUST NOT do
123	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers.** Those are managed by the `sync_leanok` phase + the review agent — never by you.
124	- **Do NOT edit other chapters.** Even when you spot a related issue, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" instead of fixing it.
125	- **Do NOT edit `content.tex`** (the top-level blueprint file that `\input`s the chapters).
126	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
127	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
128	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
129	
130	## Reading references
131	
132	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
133	
134	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
135	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
136	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
137	
138	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
139	
140	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
141	
142	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
143	
144	Conditions for dispatch:
145	
146	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
147	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
148	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
149	
150	Dispatch (Bash, foreground, in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
151	
152	```
153	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
154	  --name reference-retriever \
155	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
156	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
157	  --write-domain 'references/**'
158	```
159	
160	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
161	
162	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
163	
164	## Workflow
165	
166	1. Read your directive completely.
167	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references.
168	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
169	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow.
170	5. **If a needed source is missing from `references/`, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and wait for it to return before drafting the affected sections.
171	6. Make the edits.
172	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`).
173	8. Write your report.
174	
175	## Logging
176	
177	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
178	
179	```markdown
180	# Blueprint Writer Report
181	
182	## Slug
183	<slug>
184	
185	## Status
186	<COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE>
187	<If INCOMPLETE: which required items could not be written and why.>
188	
189	## Target chapter
190	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
191	
192	## Changes Made
193	- **Added definition** `\definition`/`\label{def:foo}`/`\lean{Foo.bar}` — <one line on what it captures>
194	- **Added theorem** `\theorem`/`\label{thm:foo}` — <one line on statement>
195	  - Proof sketch added: <Y/N + brief shape>
196	- **Revised** `<existing label>` — <one line on what changed>
197	- ...
198	
199	## Cross-references introduced
200	- `\uses{thm:bar}` added in proof of `\thm:foo` — verify `thm:bar` exists in <chapter or this same one>
201	- ...
202	
203	## Macros needed (if any)
204	- `\foo{...}` — used in <where>; needs definition in `macros/common.tex`. NOT added by me (out of write-domain).
205	
206	## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)
207	- slug `<child-slug>`: requested `<source>`. Status: COMPLETE / NOT_FOUND / PARTIAL. Summary at `references/<slug>.md`.
208	- ...
209	
210	## Notes for Plan Agent
211	- <any inconsistency I noticed in sibling chapters that the directive didn't cover>
212	- <any structural concern: e.g. "the chapter is now 800 lines, consider splitting">
213	- <any reference I had to guess at because the directive's reference section was incomplete>
214	
215	## Strategy-modifying findings
216	<populate this section ONLY if writing the chapter surfaced a need to
217	change the project's strategy itself — not just the chapter content.
218	Examples:
219	- A definition you were asked to write turns out to require a property
220	  that the strategy assumes is automatic but is not.
221	- A theorem you sketched is provable as stated, but its consequence
222	  used elsewhere in STRATEGY.md does not follow from it.
223	- A reference cited as authoritative actually requires a different
224	  setup than the strategy assumes.
225	
226	When this section is non-empty, the plan agent must update STRATEGY.md
227	before any further Lean work this iteration. Do NOT silently adapt the
228	chapter to paper over a strategy-level issue — surface it here.>
229	```
230	
231	## Return Value
232	
233	Your final assistant message:
234	
235	- One line: `<slug>: COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE — <one-sentence outcome>`
236	- The path to your full report.
237	
238	Keep the inline return short. The plan agent reads the full report.
239

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.

1	\chapter{The relative cotangent presheaf}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the relative cotangent \emph{presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$ of a morphism of schemes $f \colon X \to S$, starting from the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ already available in Mathlib. The output is a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules whose sections on each open $V \subseteq X$ are the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$.
5	
6	The downstream role of this chapter is to characterise smoothness of $f$ in terms of pointwise local freeness of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on an affine basis of $X$. This characterisation is what unlocks the dimension hypothesis on the Jacobian: the smoothness of the curve $C \to \Spec k$ in the Jacobian definition is the special case $n = 1$ of the criterion below, and the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ that appears in the smoothness statement is read off through the same criterion.
7	
8	\section{The relative cotangent presheaf}
9	
10	\begin{definition}
11	
12	\leanok
13	[Relative cotangent presheaf of a morphism]
14	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
15	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
16	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes. The inverse-image presheaf of rings $f^{-1}\struct{S}$ on $X$ is paired with $\struct{X}$ by the canonical adjunction-transpose $f^{-1}\struct{S} \to \struct{X}$ of the comorphism $f^{\sharp}\colon\struct{S}\to f_{*}\struct{X}$. Applying the presheaf-of-modules K\"ahler differential construction $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$ to this map of presheaves of commutative rings produces a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules on $X$, the \emph{relative cotangent presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$.
17	\end{definition}
18	
19	\begin{lemma}
20	
21	\leanok
22	[Sections of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on an open]
23	  \label{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
24	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler}
25	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
26	  For every open $V \subseteq X$, the sections of the relative cotangent presheaf are, by construction, the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$:
27	  \[
28	    \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{\struct{X}(V) / (f^{-1}\struct{S})(V)}.
29	  \]
30	  In particular, on an affine open $V = \Spec B \subseteq X$ whose image lies in an affine open $U = \Spec A \subseteq S$, the right-hand side coincides with the classical ring-theoretic $\Omega_{B/A}$.
31	\end{lemma}
32	
33	\begin{proof}
34	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
35	  \leanok
36	  By definition of $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$: the construction is built object-wise to be the Mathlib \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} module of the ring map at each open. The identification is therefore by \texttt{rfl} after unfolding the definition. The affine specialisation is the standard identification of the inverse-image $(f^{-1}\struct{S})(V)$ with the structure-sheaf section $\struct{S}(U)$ along the affine chart $V = \Spec B \to U = \Spec A$.
37	\end{proof}
38	
39	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level $\Omega_{B/A}$]
40	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
41	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ together with the universal derivation $\mathrm{d} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$. The construction of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on each open is, by definition, this Mathlib module applied at the ring level of the presheaf, so all ring-level Mathlib lemmas about $\Omega_{B/A}$ transfer verbatim under Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}.
42	\end{remark}
43	
44	\section{Smoothness criterion for $\Omega_{X/S}$}
45	
46	The main result of this chapter is the textbook criterion identifying smoothness of a locally-of-finite-presentation morphism with local freeness of its relative cotangent presheaf on an affine basis. The criterion is the global version of the algebraic-side equivalence between \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension} and the existence of an $\Omega_{B/A}$-basis of the prescribed rank.
47	
48	\begin{theorem}
49	
50	\leanok
51	[Smoothness in terms of $\Omega$]
52	  \label{thm:smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
53	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
54	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
55	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a locally-of-finite-presentation morphism of schemes and let $n \in \mathbb{N}$. Then $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ if and only if for every point $x \in X$ there exists an affine open $U \subseteq X$ with $x \in U$ such that the section module
56	  \[
57	    M_U \;:=\; \Omega_{X/S}(U) \;=\; (\mathrm{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,f).\mathrm{presheaf}(U)
58	  \]
59	  is a free $\struct{X}(U)$-module of rank $n$.
60	\end{theorem}
61	
62	\begin{proof}
63	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
64	  \leanok
65	  We prove the two directions separately. Throughout, when $U \subseteq X$ is affine with image inside an affine open $V \subseteq S$ we write $A := \struct{S}(V)$ and $B := \struct{X}(U)$, with induced algebra map $A \to B$ from $f^{\sharp}$.
66	
67	  \emph{Forward direction. $\Rightarrow$ smooth $\Rightarrow$ locally free of rank $n$.} Assume $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$. By the algebra-to-scheme bridge \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.isSmoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} [verified] (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth}), for every $x \in X$ one can find an affine chart $U \subseteq X$ containing $x$ over an affine $V \subseteq S$ such that the induced algebra map $A \to B$ satisfies \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n~A~B$. On such a chart, Mathlib supplies two pieces that close the forward direction:
68	  \begin{itemize}
69	    \item \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.basis\_kaehlerDifferential} [verified] (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}) provides a free $B$-basis of $\Omega_{B/A}$;
70	    \item \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} [verified] (same file) pins its rank to $n$.
71	  \end{itemize}
72	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, the section module $M_U = \Omega_{X/S}(U)$ is precisely $\Omega_{B/A}$, so the basis transfers verbatim and exhibits $M_U$ as a free $\struct{X}(U)$-module of rank $n$. As $x \in X$ was arbitrary, the right-hand side of the equivalence holds.
73	
74	  \emph{Converse direction. $\Leftarrow$ locally free of rank $n$ $\Rightarrow$ smooth.} Assume that $f$ is locally of finite presentation and that for every $x \in X$ there is an affine open $U \subseteq X$ containing $x$ on which $M_U = \Omega_{B/A}$ is a free $B$-module of rank $n$. By Mathlib's standard-smooth-via-basis criterion
75	  \[
76	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff\_exists\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
77	  \]
78	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}), the existence of a $B$-basis of $\Omega_{B/A}$ is equivalent to $B$ being standard smooth as an $A$-algebra, \emph{conditional on the formal-smoothness hypothesis} \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent A B)} (i.e.\ vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology). The genuine deformation-theoretic content of the converse is this $H^{1}$-cotangent vanishing; the project's local-freeness hypothesis combined with the locally-of-finite-presentation hypothesis on $f$ supplies the required vanishing on each affine chart. Combined with
79	  \[
80	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.iff\_of\_isStandardSmooth} \quad [\text{verified}]
81	  \]
82	  (same file), which packages standard-smooth plus the rank condition on $\Omega_{B/A}$ into \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n~A~B$, this yields the algebra-side smoothness conclusion on every affine chart of $X$.
83	
84	  \emph{Local-to-global.} The algebra-to-scheme bridge \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.isSmoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} [verified], in the reverse direction from its forward use above, lifts the affine-chart-local property \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n~A~B$ at every point to the global statement that $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$. This is the textbook Jacobian criterion of Stacks Tag~02HW (and Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.7).
85	
86	  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} The five \texttt{[verified]} closure lemmas the proof relies on are
87	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.basis\_kaehlerDifferential},
88	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential},
89	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff\_exists\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential},
90	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.iff\_of\_isStandardSmooth},
91	  and the algebra-to-scheme bridge \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.isSmoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff}.
92	\end{proof}
93	
94	\section{Content out of autonomous-loop scope}
95	
96	The following content was developed in earlier iterations of the project and trimmed in iter-117 because the required Mathlib infrastructure is absent from the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227} and the bridge work is outside the scope of the autonomous loop. Each item lists the trimmed concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.
97	
98	\begin{itemize}
99	  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$.} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf $\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}$ on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis $\Rightarrow$ sheaf on $X$'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is hand-rolled work outside autonomous-loop scope. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.
100	
101	  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence.} For a composition of morphisms $X \to Y \to S$, there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
102	  \[
103	    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
104	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
105	  \]
106	  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.
107	
108	  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section.} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section $s \colon S \to X$: if $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and $s$ is a section, then $s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$. The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}$, which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Out of autonomous-loop scope. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.
109	
110	  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity.} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve $C/k$,
111	  \[
112	    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),
113	  \]
114	  both sides being equal to the genus $g(C)$. Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf, no trace morphism for proper morphisms, and no Zariski coherent cohomology of $\struct{X}$-modules, so the equality cannot be expressed within the autonomous-loop's Mathlib snapshot. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~III.7 and Stacks Tag~0FVU.
115	\end{itemize}

13	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
14	
15	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
16	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
17	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
18	`Ω` on affine opens.
19	
20	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
21	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
22	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
23	
24	## References
25	
26	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
38	
39	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
40	
41	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
42	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
43	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
44	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
45	
46	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
47	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
48	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
49	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
50	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
51	    f.c
52	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
53	
54	/-- Localisation-compatibility identity: on each open `V ⊆ X`, the sections
55	of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over `V` are *definitionally* the
56	Kähler differential module of the ring map
57	`(f⁻¹ O_S)(V) → O_X(V)`. -/
58	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
59	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
60	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
61	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
62	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
63	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
64	  rfl
65	
66	/-- Smoothness of a finite-presentation morphism is equivalent to the
67	relative differential presheaf being locally free of the given rank on
68	every affine open.
69	
70	The forward direction is the Jacobian criterion via
71	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.basis_kaehlerDifferential`
72	on affine charts; the converse follows from
73	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`. -/
74	theorem smooth_iff_locally_free_omega (f : X ⟶ S)
75	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (n : ℕ) :
76	    AlgebraicGeometry.IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension n f ↔
77	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : X.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
78	        let R := X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
79	        let M := (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op U)
80	        Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
81	  sorry
82	
83	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

\section{Smoothness criterion for $\Omega_{X/S}$}

The main result of this chapter is the textbook criterion identifying smoothness of a locally-of-finite-presentation morphism with local freeness of its relative cotangent presheaf on an affine basis. The criterion is the global version of the algebraic-side equivalence between \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension} and the existence of an $\Omega_{B/A}$-basis of the prescribed rank.

\begin{theorem}

\leanok
[Smoothness in terms of $\Omega$]
  \label{thm:smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_iff_locally_free_omega}
  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a locally-of-finite-presentation morphism of schemes and let $n \in \mathbb{N}$. Then $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ if and only if for every point $x \in X$ there exists an affine open $U \subseteq X$ with $x \in U$ such that the section module
  \[
    M_U \;:=\; \Omega_{X/S}(U) \;=\; (\mathrm{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,f).\mathrm{presheaf}(U)
  \]
  is a free $\struct{X}(U)$-module of rank $n$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
  \leanok
  We prove the two directions separately. Throughout, when $U \subseteq X$ is affine with image inside an affine open $V \subseteq S$ we write $A := \struct{S}(V)$ and $B := \struct{X}(U)$, with induced algebra map $A \to B$ from $f^{\sharp}$.

  \emph{Forward direction. $\Rightarrow$ smooth $\Rightarrow$ locally free of rank $n$.} Assume $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$. By the algebra-to-scheme bridge \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.isSmoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} [verified] (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth}), for every $x \in X$ one can find an affine chart $U \subseteq X$ containing $x$ over an affine $V \subseteq S$ such that the induced algebra map $A \to B$ satisfies \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n~A~B$. On such a chart, Mathlib supplies two pieces that close the forward direction:
  \begin{itemize}
    \item \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.basis\_kaehlerDifferential} [verified] (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}) provides a free $B$-basis of $\Omega_{B/A}$;
    \item \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} [verified] (same file) pins its rank to $n$.
  \end{itemize}
  By Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, the section module $M_U = \Omega_{X/S}(U)$ is precisely $\Omega_{B/A}$, so the basis transfers verbatim and exhibits $M_U$ as a free $\struct{X}(U)$-module of rank $n$. As $x \in X$ was arbitrary, the right-hand side of the equivalence holds.

  \emph{Converse direction. $\Leftarrow$ locally free of rank $n$ $\Rightarrow$ smooth.} Assume that $f$ is locally of finite presentation and that for every $x \in X$ there is an affine open $U \subseteq X$ containing $x$ on which $M_U = \Omega_{B/A}$ is a free $B$-module of rank $n$. By Mathlib's standard-smooth-via-basis criterion
  \[
    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff\_exists\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
  \]
  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}), the existence of a $B$-basis of $\Omega_{B/A}$ is equivalent to $B$ being standard smooth as an $A$-algebra, \emph{conditional on the formal-smoothness hypothesis} \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent A B)} (i.e.\ vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology). The genuine deformation-theoretic content of the converse is this $H^{1}$-cotangent vanishing; the project's local-freeness hypothesis combined with the locally-of-finite-presentation hypothesis on $f$ supplies the required vanishing on each affine chart. Combined with
  \[
    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.iff\_of\_isStandardSmooth} \quad [\text{verified}]
  \]
  (same file), which packages standard-smooth plus the rank condition on $\Omega_{B/A}$ into \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n~A~B$, this yields the algebra-side smoothness conclusion on every affine chart of $X$.

  \emph{Local-to-global.} The algebra-to-scheme bridge \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.isSmoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} [verified], in the reverse direction from its forward use above, lifts the affine-chart-local property \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n~A~B$ at every point to the global statement that $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$. This is the textbook Jacobian criterion of Stacks Tag~02HW (and Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.7).

  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} The five \texttt{[verified]} closure lemmas the proof relies on are
  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.basis\_kaehlerDifferential},
  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential},
  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff\_exists\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential},
  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.iff\_of\_isStandardSmooth},
  and the algebra-to-scheme bridge \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.isSmoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff}.
\end{proof}

\section{Smoothness criterion for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (forward direction)}

The forward direction of the textbook smoothness criterion identifies a necessary condition on $\Omega_{X/S}$: every point of $X$ admits an affine open over which the section module is free of the prescribed rank. This is the global version of the algebraic-side fact that an \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~algebra has a free K\"ahler differential module of rank $n$. The converse implication (a local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ alone implying smoothness) is \emph{false}; see Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.

\begin{theorem}[Smooth implies $\Omega$ locally free]
  \label{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega}
  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes and let $n \in \mathbb{N}$. If $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$, then for every point $x \in X$ there exists an affine open $U \subseteq X$ with $x \in U$ such that the section module
  \[
    M_U \;:=\; \Omega_{X/S}(U) \;=\; (\mathrm{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,f).\mathrm{presheaf}(U)
  \]
  is a free $\struct{X}(U)$-module of rank $n$.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
  Fix $x \in X$. When $U \subseteq X$ is affine with image inside an affine open $V \subseteq S$ we write $A := \struct{S}(V)$ and $B := \struct{X}(U)$, with induced algebra map $A \to B$ from $f^{\sharp}$.

  \emph{Step 1: extract a standard-smooth chart at $x$.} The auto-generated \texttt{mk\_iff} bridge
  \[
    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} \quad [\text{verified}]
  \]
  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth}; produced by the \texttt{@[mk\_iff]} attribute on the class \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}) unfolds the hypothesis \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$f$ into: for every $x \in X$, there exist affine opens $V \subseteq S$ and $U \subseteq X$ with $x \in U$ and $U \leq f^{-1}V$, such that the induced algebra map $(f.\mathrm{appLE}\,V\,U\,e).\mathrm{hom}\colon A \to B$ satisfies \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.

  \emph{Step 2: pass to plain standard-smooth.} The lemma
  \[
    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} \quad [\text{verified}]
  \]
  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmooth}) yields \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$ from the relative-dimension-tagged form, unlocking the cotangent-module machinery in the next two steps.

  \emph{Step 3: free K\"ahler module on the chart.} The Mathlib instance
  \[
    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
  \]
  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}) synthesises \texttt{Module.Free}~$B$~$\Omega_{B/A}$ from \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$.

  \emph{Step 4: pin the rank.} The theorem
  \[
    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
  \]
  (same file as Step 3) gives $\rank_B\,\Omega_{B/A} = n$ under the hypotheses \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.

  \emph{Side condition on \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$.} The hypothesis $x \in U$ guarantees that the affine open $U \subseteq X$ is non-empty, hence its section ring $B = \struct{X}(U)$ is non-zero, supplying \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$.

  \emph{Step 5: transfer the module structure back to $M_U$.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, the section module $M_U = \Omega_{X/S}(U)$ is definitionally equal to $\Omega_{B/A}$, so the free-of-rank-$n$ conclusion from Steps 3--4 transfers verbatim. This exhibits $M_U$ as a free $\struct{X}(U) = B$-module of rank $n$. As $x \in X$ was arbitrary, the statement follows.

  This is the forward direction of the Jacobian criterion: Stacks Tag~02G1 (and Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2 in the Noetherian case).

  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} The five \texttt{[verified]} closure pieces the proof relies on are
  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} (Step 1),
  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} (Step 2),
  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 3),
  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 4),
  and the project-local Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj} (Step 5).
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}[Lean naming]
  \label{rem:smooth_class_naming}
  The Lean class for ``smooth of relative dimension $n$'' is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension}; the spelling \texttt{IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension} that appeared in earlier iterations of this project is a deprecated alias in Mathlib \texttt{b80f227}. The \texttt{\textbackslash lean\{...\}} hint above names the non-deprecated form.
\end{remark>

\section{Converse direction --- out of autonomous-loop scope}
\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}

The reverse implication
\[
  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
\]
is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that $f$ is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of $\Omega$ alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.

\begin{remark}[Explicit counterexample]
  \label{rem:converse_counterexample}
  Let $k$ be a field and consider the morphism $f \colon \Spec k \to \Spec k[t]$ induced by the surjection $k[t] \twoheadrightarrow k$ sending $t \mapsto 0$. Then $f$ is locally of finite presentation, its source $\Spec k$ has a unique affine open (itself), and the K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{k/k[t]} = 0$ is free of rank $0$ (the trivial \texttt{Module.Basis} indexed by the empty set witnesses this). Yet $f$ is the closed immersion of a non-open point of $\Spec k[t]$, hence is not flat and therefore not smooth, so \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$0$~$f$ fails. The local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ is therefore strictly weaker than smoothness, and the converse implication of the iff form fails on this very example.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[The Mathlib converse lemma and its hypotheses]
  \label{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}
  Mathlib does provide a converse-direction lemma at the ring level,
  \[
    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
  \]
  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}). Its three hypotheses make the missing content explicit:
  \begin{itemize}
    \item \texttt{Algebra.FinitePresentation}~$A$~$B$;
    \item \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} --- vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology, i.e.\ formal smoothness;
    \item a basis $b \colon \texttt{Module.Basis}~I~B~\Omega_{B/A}$ whose range lies in the image of the universal derivation, $\mathrm{Set.range}\,(\texttt{Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.D}~A~B)$.
  \end{itemize}
  None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``$\Omega$ locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~\ref{rem:converse_counterexample} would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is out of autonomous-loop scope.
\end{remark}

\begin{remark}[Cross-reference to Stacks 02G1]
  \label{rem:stacks_02G1}
  The standard textbook biconditional ``$f$ is smooth of relative dimension $d$ iff $f$ is flat, locally of finite presentation, and $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $d$'' is Stacks Tag~02G1 (and is the Noetherian-case Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2). The flatness conjunct is precisely what is missing from a hypothesis that only asserts local freeness of $\Omega$; supplying it amounts to the deformation-theoretic content discussed in Remark~\ref{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}. The forward implication proved as Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} above corresponds to the easy direction of the same tag.
\end{remark}

1	\chapter{The relative cotangent presheaf}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the relative cotangent \emph{presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$ of a morphism of schemes $f \colon X \to S$, starting from the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ already available in Mathlib. The output is a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules whose sections on each open $V \subseteq X$ are the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$.
5	
6	The downstream role of this chapter is to provide the \emph{forward direction} of the standard smoothness criterion: smoothness of $f$ implies pointwise local freeness of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on an affine basis of $X$. This is the Jacobian-criterion direction and is what connects to the dimension hypothesis on the Jacobian: the smoothness of the curve $C \to \Spec k$ in the Jacobian definition is the special case $n = 1$, and the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ is read off from the rank of $\Omega$ via the same forward implication. The converse implication is \emph{mathematically false} as stated and is documented separately in Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
7	
8	\section{The relative cotangent presheaf}
9	
10	\begin{definition}
11	
12	\leanok
13	[Relative cotangent presheaf of a morphism]
14	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
15	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
16	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes. The inverse-image presheaf of rings $f^{-1}\struct{S}$ on $X$ is paired with $\struct{X}$ by the canonical adjunction-transpose $f^{-1}\struct{S} \to \struct{X}$ of the comorphism $f^{\sharp}\colon\struct{S}\to f_{*}\struct{X}$. Applying the presheaf-of-modules K\"ahler differential construction $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$ to this map of presheaves of commutative rings produces a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules on $X$, the \emph{relative cotangent presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$.
17	\end{definition}
18	
19	\begin{lemma}
20	
21	\leanok
22	[Sections of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on an open]
23	  \label{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
24	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler}
25	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
26	  For every open $V \subseteq X$, the sections of the relative cotangent presheaf are, by construction, the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$:
27	  \[
28	    \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{\struct{X}(V) / (f^{-1}\struct{S})(V)}.
29	  \]
30	  In particular, on an affine open $V = \Spec B \subseteq X$ whose image lies in an affine open $U = \Spec A \subseteq S$, the right-hand side coincides with the classical ring-theoretic $\Omega_{B/A}$.
31	\end{lemma}
32	
33	\begin{proof}
34	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
35	  \leanok
36	  By definition of $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$: the construction is built object-wise to be the Mathlib \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} module of the ring map at each open. The identification is therefore by \texttt{rfl} after unfolding the definition. The affine specialisation is the standard identification of the inverse-image $(f^{-1}\struct{S})(V)$ with the structure-sheaf section $\struct{S}(U)$ along the affine chart $V = \Spec B \to U = \Spec A$.
37	\end{proof}
38	
39	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level $\Omega_{B/A}$]
40	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
41	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ together with the universal derivation $\mathrm{d} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$. The construction of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on each open is, by definition, this Mathlib module applied at the ring level of the presheaf, so all ring-level Mathlib lemmas about $\Omega_{B/A}$ transfer verbatim under Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}.
42	\end{remark}
43	
44	\section{Smoothness criterion for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (forward direction)}
45	
46	The forward direction of the textbook smoothness criterion identifies a necessary condition on $\Omega_{X/S}$: every point of $X$ admits an affine open over which the section module is free of the prescribed rank. This is the global version of the algebraic-side fact that an \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~algebra has a free K\"ahler differential module of rank $n$. The converse implication (a local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ alone implying smoothness) is \emph{false}; see Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
47	
48	\begin{theorem}[Smooth implies $\Omega$ locally free]
49	  \label{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
50	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega}
51	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
52	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes and let $n \in \mathbb{N}$. If $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$, then for every point $x \in X$ there exists an affine open $U \subseteq X$ with $x \in U$ such that the section module
53	  \[
54	    M_U \;:=\; \Omega_{X/S}(U) \;=\; (\mathrm{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,f).\mathrm{presheaf}(U)
55	  \]
56	  is a free $\struct{X}(U)$-module of rank $n$.
57	\end{theorem}
58	
59	\begin{proof}
60	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
61	  Fix $x \in X$. When $U \subseteq X$ is affine with image inside an affine open $V \subseteq S$ we write $A := \struct{S}(V)$ and $B := \struct{X}(U)$, with induced algebra map $A \to B$ from $f^{\sharp}$.
62	
63	  \emph{Step 1: extract a standard-smooth chart at $x$.} The auto-generated \texttt{mk\_iff} bridge
64	  \[
65	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} \quad [\text{verified}]
66	  \]
67	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth}; produced by the \texttt{@[mk\_iff]} attribute on the class \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}) unfolds the hypothesis \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$f$ into: for every $x \in X$, there exist affine opens $V \subseteq S$ and $U \subseteq X$ with $x \in U$ and $U \leq f^{-1}V$, such that the induced algebra map $(f.\mathrm{appLE}\,V\,U\,e).\mathrm{hom}\colon A \to B$ satisfies \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.
68	
69	  \emph{Step 2: pass to plain standard-smooth.} The lemma
70	  \[
71	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} \quad [\text{verified}]
72	  \]
73	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmooth}) yields \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$ from the relative-dimension-tagged form, unlocking the cotangent-module machinery in the next two steps.
74	
75	  \emph{Step 3: free K\"ahler module on the chart.} The Mathlib instance
76	  \[
77	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
78	  \]
79	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}) synthesises \texttt{Module.Free}~$B$~$\Omega_{B/A}$ from \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$.
80	
81	  \emph{Step 4: pin the rank.} The theorem
82	  \[
83	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
84	  \]
85	  (same file as Step 3) gives $\rank_B\,\Omega_{B/A} = n$ under the hypotheses \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.
86	
87	  \emph{Side condition on \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$.} The hypothesis $x \in U$ guarantees that the affine open $U \subseteq X$ is non-empty, hence its section ring $B = \struct{X}(U)$ is non-zero, supplying \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$.
88	
89	  \emph{Step 5: transfer the module structure back to $M_U$.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, the section module $M_U = \Omega_{X/S}(U)$ is definitionally equal to $\Omega_{B/A}$, so the free-of-rank-$n$ conclusion from Steps 3--4 transfers verbatim. This exhibits $M_U$ as a free $\struct{X}(U) = B$-module of rank $n$. As $x \in X$ was arbitrary, the statement follows.
90	
91	  This is the forward direction of the Jacobian criterion: Stacks Tag~02G1 (and Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2 in the Noetherian case).
92	
93	  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} The five \texttt{[verified]} closure pieces the proof relies on are
94	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} (Step 1),
95	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} (Step 2),
96	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 3),
97	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 4),
98	  and the project-local Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj} (Step 5).
99	\end{proof}
100	
101	\begin{remark}[Lean naming]
102	  \label{rem:smooth_class_naming}
103	  The Lean class for ``smooth of relative dimension $n$'' is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension}; the spelling \texttt{IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension} that appeared in earlier iterations of this project is a deprecated alias in Mathlib \texttt{b80f227}. The \texttt{\textbackslash lean\{...\}} hint above names the non-deprecated form.
104	\end{remark}
105	
106	\section{Converse direction --- out of autonomous-loop scope}
107	\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}
108	
109	The reverse implication
110	\[
111	  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
112	  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
113	\]
114	is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that $f$ is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of $\Omega$ alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.
115	
116	\begin{remark}[Explicit counterexample]
117	  \label{rem:converse_counterexample}
118	  Let $k$ be a field and consider the morphism $f \colon \Spec k \to \Spec k[t]$ induced by the surjection $k[t] \twoheadrightarrow k$ sending $t \mapsto 0$. Then $f$ is locally of finite presentation, its source $\Spec k$ has a unique affine open (itself), and the K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{k/k[t]} = 0$ is free of rank $0$ (the trivial \texttt{Module.Basis} indexed by the empty set witnesses this). Yet $f$ is the closed immersion of a non-open point of $\Spec k[t]$, hence is not flat and therefore not smooth, so \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$0$~$f$ fails. The local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ is therefore strictly weaker than smoothness, and the converse implication of the iff form fails on this very example.
119	\end{remark}
120	
121	\begin{remark}[The Mathlib converse lemma and its hypotheses]
122	  \label{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}
123	  Mathlib does provide a converse-direction lemma at the ring level,
124	  \[
125	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
126	  \]
127	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}). Its three hypotheses make the missing content explicit:
128	  \begin{itemize}
129	    \item \texttt{Algebra.FinitePresentation}~$A$~$B$;
130	    \item \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} --- vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology, i.e.\ formal smoothness;
131	    \item a basis $b \colon \texttt{Module.Basis}~I~B~\Omega_{B/A}$ whose range lies in the image of the universal derivation, $\mathrm{Set.range}\,(\texttt{Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.D}~A~B)$.
132	  \end{itemize}
133	  None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``$\Omega$ locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~\ref{rem:converse_counterexample} would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is out of autonomous-loop scope.
134	\end{remark}
135	
136	\begin{remark}[Cross-reference to Stacks 02G1]
137	  \label{rem:stacks_02G1}
138	  The standard textbook biconditional ``$f$ is smooth of relative dimension $d$ iff $f$ is flat, locally of finite presentation, and $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $d$'' is Stacks Tag~02G1 (and is the Noetherian-case Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2). The flatness conjunct is precisely what is missing from a hypothesis that only asserts local freeness of $\Omega$; supplying it amounts to the deformation-theoretic content discussed in Remark~\ref{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}. The forward implication proved as Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} above corresponds to the easy direction of the same tag.
139	\end{remark}
140	
141	\section{Content out of autonomous-loop scope}
142	
143	The following content was developed in earlier iterations of the project and trimmed in iter-117 because the required Mathlib infrastructure is absent from the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227} and the bridge work is outside the scope of the autonomous loop. Each item lists the trimmed concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.
144	
145	\begin{itemize}
146	  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$.} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf $\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}$ on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis $\Rightarrow$ sheaf on $X$'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is hand-rolled work outside autonomous-loop scope. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.
147	
148	  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence.} For a composition of morphisms $X \to Y \to S$, there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
149	  \[
150	    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
151	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
152	  \]
153	  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.
154	
155	  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section.} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section $s \colon S \to X$: if $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and $s$ is a section, then $s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$. The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}$, which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Out of autonomous-loop scope. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.
156	
157	  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity.} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve $C/k$,
158	  \[
159	    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),
160	  \]
161	  both sides being equal to the genus $g(C)$. Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf, no trace morphism for proper morphisms, and no Zariski coherent cohomology of $\struct{X}$-modules, so the equality cannot be expressed within the autonomous-loop's Mathlib snapshot. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~III.7 and Stacks Tag~0FVU.
162	\end{itemize}
163
